key_insight: BatteryMat combines fast machine-learning screening with force-field and DFT validation to rank lithium-ion cathode candidates while preserving thermodynamic consistency.
engineering_relevance: The framework can accelerate cathode materials discovery by reducing the number of expensive DFT calculations needed to evaluate large materials databases.
method: ALIGNN first screens lithium-containing structures in JARVIS-DFT and Alexandria for candidate average voltages.
Promising hits are validated with ALIGNN-FF delithiation profiles and automated supercell DFT using PBE+U or optB88-vdW+U.
The pipeline automatically selects the exchange-correlation functional by space group and recomputes the Li-metal reference consistently in the same basis.
evidence_signal: ALIGNN was trained on 7,610 ALIGNN-FF voltages and achieved MAE 0.17 V with R² = 0.94 against the force-field labels; DFT validation on commercial cathodes matched experimental average voltage within 0.3 V.
watch_item: The ML stage is a surrogate of the force-field protocol rather than direct DFT or experimental voltage, so candidate rankings still require DFT confirmation.
paper_title: BatteryMat: a hierarchical machine-learning and DFT framework for average-voltage screening of lithium-ion cathode materials
